title: Retrieval Review: User-Centered Interface Design
summary: Retrieval Review: User-Centered Interface Design retrieves prior skills in an original changed community-service redesign brief scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply design user goals, design information hierarchy, design spacing alignment, design color typography, design consistency feedback, design prototype test in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed community-service redesign brief artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey redesign here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the survey design system here. */

content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Retrieval Review: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Retrieval Review: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Retrieval Review: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 1: the stakeholder feature list is equivalent to evidence about user needs.

Changed community-service redesign brief case 1: separate requests from research evidence, state prioritized user tasks and constraints, and define observable success before choosing interface features.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 1: treat every requested feature as equally urgent so no stakeholder preference is challenged.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 3: whitespace is unused room that should be filled to maximize value.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 3: map repeated relationships to a small spacing scale, align related edges, and test grouping at narrow width, zoom, and changed content length.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 3: choose a unique margin for each element so every section has a custom personality.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 5: consistency means every component must look identical regardless of purpose.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 5: standardize patterns by purpose and state, preserve meaningful distinctions, and verify immediate feedback plus recovery across the complete task.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 5: give every message the same blue treatment so users learn one universal state color.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 6: a polished mockup is proof that users can complete the intended task.

Changed community-service redesign brief case 6: choose fidelity from the risk being tested, observe representative tasks, record behavior rather than preference alone, and revise the design decision.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 6: ask only whether participants like the colors and treat positive opinions as task success.
